stem: In right triangle PQR, PQ = 9, QR = 40, and PR = 41. Triangle PQR is similar to triangle XYZ, where Q corresponds to Y and R corresponds to Z. What is the value of tan Z?
rationale: Since PR = 41 is the longest side, it is the hypotenuse, so the right angle is at Q. In triangle PQR, the tangent of angle R is the ratio of the side opposite R (which is PQ = 9) to the side adjacent to R (which is QR = 40), giving tan R = 9/40. Because the triangles are similar with R↔Z, tan Z = tan R = 9/40.
